data.title: Civeo prices $100M convertible notes, wins six-year Canada contract renewal
data.description:
• Civeo (CVEO) priced a private offering of $100M of 4.50% convertible senior notes due 2031, and announced a six-year contract renewal for workforce accommodation and hospitality services in Western Canada.
  • The convertible notes, offered to qualified institutional buyers, will pay 4.50% annual interest, maturing on Aug. 1, 2031, unless earlier repurchased, redeemed, or converted. The initial conversion rate is 24.6840 shares per $1,000 principal amount, equivalent to a conversion price of about $40.51 per share, representing a 20% premium to Civeo's July 1 closing price of $33.76.
  • The notes are expected to close on July 7. Initial purchasers have a 13-day option to buy up to an additional $15M of notes.
  • Separately, one of Civeo's Western Canada joint ventures secured a six-year contract renewal to continue providing workforce accommodations and hospitality services.
  • The renewed agreement extends through June 30, 2032, replacing existing arrangements that were due to expire in 2027.

data.title: EU chip industry squeezed by China, U.S. dependence
data.description:
The European Union's semiconductor sector is under increasing pressure from Chinese export controls, dependence on U.S. technology, and structural weaknesses in its domestic chip industry, according to an EU-funded report.

"From the perspective of European semiconductor firms and RTOs, the future of technology transfer regimes looks bleak," the report by the European Union Institute for Security Studies and Institut Montaigne said.

The report said the EU relies heavily on the U.S. for critical semiconductor technologies, including electronic design automation software and advanced chip technologies, while China remains a key market and supplier of critical raw materials such as rare earths and magnets. It warned that escalating U.S.-China technology tensions leave European firms vulnerable to policy decisions beyond their control.

"While Beijing still appears to be the biggest threat, dependence on Washington seems to have become of much greater concern under the second Trump administration," co-author Joris Teer told Reuters.

The report also cited persistently high energy prices, limited private capital investment and the decline of Europe's chip-consuming industries as factors undermining the sector's competitiveness.

It pointed that Europe is unlikely to achieve full semiconductor self-sufficiency and should instead pursue "strategic interdependence" by reducing excessive dependencies while deepening cooperation with trusted partners. The report recommended building on existing strengths, particularly ASML's leadership in chipmaking equipment, investing across the semiconductor value chain and preparing for a more fragmented global technology landscape.

The European Commission's proposed Chips Act 2.0 aims to boost demand for EU-made chips and strengthen allied semiconductor supply chains.

data.title: Elicio Therapeutics drops 5%, prices $15M direct offering
Apple prepping 10M foldable iPhones for this year, eyes 5 new models: Nikkei
data.description:
• Apple (AAPL) is planning an ambitious iPhone launch for the second half of this year and first half of 2027 with at least five new models and a larger volume of folding handsets than previously expected, as it looks to gain market share amid an industrywide component supply crunch, Nikkei Asia reported.
  • The company has asked suppliers to prepare to manufacture about 10 million foldable iPhones this year, up from an earlier forecast of 7 million to 8 million units, the report said.
  • This push coincides with an accelerating race among smartphone makers to broaden their foldable offerings—a high-growth segment currently led by Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) and Chinese competitors like Huawei.

data.title: Bayer announces consolidation of U.S. glyphosate business into newly formed 'Ruveon LLC'
Alibaba's Ant Group backs Zeroth Robotics in latest AI venture
data.description:
Alibaba-affiliate (BABA) Ant Group is ramping up its move into humanoid robots. Ant has led a 500M yuan ($73.58M) funding round in humanoid robotics company Zeroth, the start-up said on Thursday.

It’s the 12th company in the sector that Ant has invested in since the beginning of 2025, according to CNBC analysis of PitchBook data. The investments tracked by CNBC range from humanoid robotics companies Galaxea and Unitree, to parts and software start-ups such as Linkerbot, Hypershell, and Genrobot AI.

Ant has released an AI and robotics-friendly version of its Alipay mobile payments service, which is an area Zeroth said it would like to cooperate in. Monolith, Geely Capital, 37 Interactive Entertainment, and Hua Capital also participated in Zeroth’s latest funding round. The pre-Series A raise brings total funds raised to 1B yuan, the report said.

The start-up’s founder, Guo Renjie, told CNBC that Zeroth focused on securing companies with experience in industries such as smartphone chips. He said the company’s robots currently use chips from Horizon Robotics.

Zeroth Robotics, known in China as Suzhou JoyIn Intelligent Technology, was founded in late 2024. The Ant Group-led deal comes as interest in humanoid robots grows in China. Nvidia (NVDA) on Monday announced it was hiring for several robotics roles based in Beijing, Shanghai, and Shenzhen.

data.title: Australia slips into unexpected AUD 3.02B trade deficit as exports tumble
Microsoft joins consortium to build India-Southeast Asia undersea AI cable - report
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) has joined a consortium led by Singapore-based telecom startup Lightstorm to build a 3,600-km undersea cable linking India with Malaysia and Singapore.

The consortium, which also includes Tata Communications, Singapore Telecommunications (SGAPY), ASEAN Cableship and Japan's NEC (NECPY), will develop the I-2SEA cable system to support AI, cloud and hyperscale workloads, a Reuters report added.

The 3,600-km cable network will feature landing stations in Machilipatnam, Andhra Pradesh, where Meta (META) and Alphabet (GOOGL) have announced data center projects.

Lightstorm Group CEO and Managing Director Amajit Gupta said that the I-2SEA cable is expected to enter service in the fourth quarter of 2029, according to the report.

Gupta said that the I Squared-backed company currently connects 19 AI and cloud zones across India through its terrestrial fiber network, with the new cable expected to expand that footprint to 29 zones.

He added that Lightstorm plans to launch an IPO in India in mid-2027 and was reportedly seeking a valuation of up to $1.5B in March.
